label: Graph transformers

node.label: Graph transformers
node.definition: A graph transformer combines attention with graph topology, structural encodings, or edge features to model long-range interaction over relational data.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Graph transformers is modeled as depending on Graph neural networks, Self-attention and feeding downstream nodes such as Drug discovery, protein & molecular design, Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning.
examples: atlas downstream link to Drug discovery, protein & molecular design
atlas downstream link to Knowledge graphs, semantic search & relational reasoning
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Graph neural networks, Self-attention.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Graph transformers has a `specializes` dependency on Graph neural networks with `common` strength.
Graph transformers is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Self-attention.
In this atlas graph, Graph transformers has a `composes` dependency on Relative position, RoPE, ALiBi & coordinate mechanisms with `common` strength.
Graph transformers are transformer architectures adapted to graph-structured inputs.
